topic: JetBrains AI Coding Tools Survey 2026: What Developers Actually Use at Work

key_points: Primary source: JetBrains AI Pulse January 2026 survey, 10,000+ developers
Covers awareness vs actual adoption gap across all major tools
Claude Code and Cursor tied at 18% work adoption
GitHub Copilot growth stalled despite 76% awareness
Analysis of JetBrains survey through competitive lens
Antigravity 120% growth rate highlighted
Enterprise vs startup adoption split covered
Claude Code's 6x year-over-year growth narrative
Secondary JetBrains report on AI workflow impact
9-in-10 developers save at least 1 hour/week from AI
Quality concerns: 40% say reviewing AI code takes more effort
Productivity paradox analysis
Market share perspective beyond JetBrains data
Startup preference for Claude Code at 75%
Enterprise preference for GitHub Copilot at 56% (10K+ employees)
Revenue and market size estimates for AI coding tools
Aggregated AI coding adoption and productivity metrics
40% of less-experienced devs: AI code looks correct but isn't reliable
Broader industry context for JetBrains findings
Historical adoption trend data

stats.fact: 90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work in January 2026 (JetBrains AI Pulse, 10,000+ developers)
GitHub Copilot: 76% awareness, 29% adoption at work — growth has stalled year-over-year
Cursor and Claude Code both at 18% work adoption, sharing second place behind GitHub Copilot
Claude Code awareness: 57% (Jan 2026) vs 49% (Sep 2025) vs 31% (Apr-Jun 2025) — fastest-growing awareness in category
Claude Code adoption at work: 18% in Jan 2026 — 1.5x from Sep 2025, 6x from ~3% in Apr-Jun 2025
Claude Code CSAT: 91%, NPS: 54 — highest product loyalty metrics in the AI coding tools market
Startups choose Claude Code at 75% adoption; enterprises (10,000+ employees) choose GitHub Copilot at 56%
JetBrains AI Assistant used regularly by 9% of developers; Junie by 5%
Chatbot usage for coding: ChatGPT 28%, Gemini 8%, Claude chatbot 7%
Nearly 9-in-10 developers save at least 1 hour/week using AI; 1-in-5 save 8+ hours/week
40% of developers with <10 years experience say AI code looks correct but isn't reliable; reviewing it takes more effort
Google Antigravity reached 6% adoption by January 2026 with 120% growth rate — fastest new entrant

angles: The adoption gap paradox: 90% use AI tools broadly but only 18% use specialized coding assistants daily — why the chasm persists
Claude Code's 6x growth story: from 3% to 18% in under a year — fastest rise in AI coding tool history
Startup vs enterprise divide: 75% Claude Code adoption at startups vs 56% GitHub Copilot at large enterprises
Product satisfaction as the new competitive moat: Claude Code's 91% CSAT while Copilot growth stalls
The productivity reality check: 9-in-10 save an hour/week, but 40% say reviewing AI code takes more effort than their own
Google Antigravity emergence: 120% growth signals genuine third-party contender arriving mid-cycle
JetBrains' own tools losing ground: AI Assistant at 9% and Junie at 5% — IDE-native tools can't compete with agentic CLI tools
keywords: GitHub Copilot vs Claude Code market share
outline: H2: Survey Methodology: JetBrains AI Pulse January 2026 (10,000+ Developers Worldwide)
H2: Overall AI Adoption: 90% Use AI at Work, But Only 18% Use Specialized Coding Tools
H2: Tool-by-Tool Breakdown: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, Chatbots, and More
H2: The Claude Code Surge: From 3% to 18% Work Adoption in Under a Year
H2: Enterprise vs Startup Split: Why Company Size Determines Which Tool Teams Choose
H2: Developer Satisfaction Rankings: Claude Code Leads on CSAT (91%) and NPS (54)
H2: Productivity Reality Check: Hours Saved vs Code Quality and Review Burden
H2: What's Coming: Google Antigravity, JetBrains Junie, and the Next Wave of Tools
